title: Ectopic pregnancy - NHS
text:
using these, but if you do you're more likely to have an ectopic pregnancy smoking increasing age – the risk is highest for pregnant women aged over 35 You can't always prevent an ectopic pregnancy, but you can reduce your risk by using a  condom when not trying for a baby to protect yourself against STIs, and by stopping smoking if you smoke.
